title: U.S. Antitank Mines Discovered in Neighborhood in Iran
summary: Photos and video verified by The New York Times show mines dispensed by cluster bombs in a village a few miles from a missile site.

title: At CPAC, the ‘America First’ Crowd Ponders Giving War a Chance
summary: The Conservative Political Action Conference has been an America First gathering for years, but with President Trump pursuing war in Iran, organizers have tried to make a shift, with mixed results.

title: House Vote Sets Up Clash With the Senate on D.H.S. Funding, Prolonging Shutdown
summary: Republicans revolted over a Senate measure to fund the Department of Homeland Security and passed a rival bill, dimming the chances of a quick end to the crisis crippling airports.

title: How Long of a Wait at Security? For Many Passengers, It Was Anyone’s Guess.
summary: As T.S.A. staff shortages continued on Friday, some airports saw interminably long wait times, while others barely had a wait at all, adding to travelers’ confusion.

title: Florida’s Immigration Crackdown, Led by DeSantis, Is Showing Cracks
summary: Some conservative sheriffs have raised concerns about the aggressive enforcement tactics that Gov. Ron DeSantis, a Republican, has embraced.

title: Stocks Keep Falling as Investor Lose Patience With the War in Iran
summary: The S&P 500 is down nearly 9 percent from a January high. The weekly losing streak is its worst in roughly four years.

title: Cannonball with Wesley Morris: ‘Love Story’ Is Actually a Horror Story
summary: The nightmare began when she said, “I do.”

title: Want More ‘Love Story’? Read These Books Inspired by the Kennedys and ’90s New York.
summary: If the TV show has you craving 1990s glam, upper-crust romance and doomed dynasties, these books have got you covered.

title: How ‘Love Story’ Gave Viewers a History Lesson on the Kennedys and ’90s NYC
summary: The FX show, which dramatizes the relationship between John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette, has inspired a legion of younger viewers to dig into the couple’s past and 1990s New York City.

title: Hegseth Strikes Two Black and Two Female Officers From Promotion List
summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s highly unusual decision to remove officers from a one-star promotion list has spurred allegations of racial and gender bias.

title: Hegseth Criticized for Removing Four Officers From Promotion List
summary: Bitter rifts have opened over the defense secretary’s campaign to reverse policies that he says are prejudiced against white officers.

title: Trump Offers More Aid to Farmers, a Key Support Bloc Hurt by Tariffs and War
summary: The president announced new loan guarantees at a White House event that appeared intended to reassure a group that has found itself caught in the crosshairs of some of his major policies.

title: Kash Patel’s Emails Circulate Online as Iran Takes Responsibility for Release
summary: What appeared to be personal emails from before Mr. Patel’s time as director were posted on a website that identified itself as Iranian but seemed to be hosted in Russia.

title: Outside Kennedy Center, Jane Fonda and Joan Baez Raise Voices in Protest
summary: The pair joined in a gathering of artists and others who denounced censorship and faulted President Trump’s growing influence over the nation’s cultural life.

title: New York City Ballet Pulls Out of Kennedy Center Performances
summary: The decision by the company, one of the most prestigious in the country, is the latest in a wave of high-profile cancellations at the center.

title: Kennedy Center Performer Asks Judge to Toss Case He Calls ‘Retaliatory’
summary: A jazz drummer who called off a performance in protest of the center’s being renamed for President Trump said the president’s allies had sued him as punishment.

title: Man Tied to Pro-Israel Extremists Accused of Targeting Palestinian Activist
summary: A man linked to a pro-Israel terrorist group was charged with making Molotov cocktails for what officials said was a plot to assassinate the New York City-based activist Nerdeen Kiswani.

title: Before the LaGuardia Crash, Why Didn’t Truck 1 Stop?
summary: After a fatal runway collision between a jet and a fire truck, one of the key questions is why the truck didn’t heed frantic calls to stop.

title: We Analyzed the Deadly Crash at LaGuardia
summary: Our graphics reporter Lazaro Gamio breaks down the second-by-second analysis leading up to the deadly plane crash at LaGuardia Airport.

title: After Being Struck by Air Canada Plane, Last Firefighter Is Released From Hospital
summary: Days after the disaster on a LaGuardia runway, two veterans of a specialized rescue unit have been released from the hospital.

title: Rep. Sam Graves, a Senior House Republican, Will Retire as Midterm Exodus Grows
summary: Representative Sam Graves, the chairman of the Transportation and Infrastructure Committee, said he would leave Congress after nearly three decades, the latest sign that the G.O.P. is bracing for big losses.

title: Hasan Piker Rallies Spark Backlash in Michigan’s Democratic Senate Primary
summary: Dr. Abdul El-Sayed’s primary opponents criticized him for inviting Hasan Piker to campaign with him. Mr. Piker is a fierce critic of Israel.

title: CPAC Considers Vance, Rubio and Life After Trump
summary: The president’s potential successors “are mortal men,” one attendee said.

title: Bank of America Pays $72.5 Million to Settle Lawsuit by Epstein Victims
summary: The suit claimed the bank had overlooked signs that Mr. Epstein’s accounts were being used to further his abuse of young women.

title: Will Iran Break Trumpism?
summary: The right-wing thinker Christopher Caldwell believes the Iran war is the end of Trumpism. Is he right? What was Trumpism in the first place?

title: Republicans Know This War Is Going Badly
summary: Never before has America arrived at the threshold of a quagmire so quickly.

title: Man Accused of Killing Loyola University Chicago Student to Remain in Jail
summary: Jose Medina, who is accused of being in the country illegally, is charged with murder in the death of Sheridan Gorman, a freshman at Loyola University Chicago.

title: Judge Agrees to Drop Charges Against Officers in Breonna Taylor’s Death
summary: The Trump administration had asked the court to dismiss the charges, describing them as an example of “weaponized federal overreach” by the Biden administration.

title: Clavicular, an Internet Narcissus, Is Arrested After Posting a Fight Video
summary: The influencer, known for promoting handsomeness, is accused of arranging a brawl between two women. Separately, the authorities are investigating a video showing him shooting an alligator.

title: Idaho Criminalizes Transgender Use of Some Bathrooms in Private Businesses
summary: The bill passed Friday by the Idaho legislature would make it a crime punishable by up to a year in prison to use a gender-designated bathroom that does not conform to a person’s sex at birth.
